text:
Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 185 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Shri Dharambir Singh
Subject: Farmer-owned Food Processing Startups
---

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES

## LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION No. *185

ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026

## FARMER-OWNED FOOD PROCESSING STARTUPS

## *185.   SHRI DHARAMBIR SINGH:

Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:

- (a) whether  the  Government  is  promoting/proposes  to  promote  farmer-owned  food  processing startups and if so, the details thereof;
- (b) whether rural entrepreneurs face access gaps in branding and marketing of their products and if so, the details thereof;
- (c) whether incubation support is available at the district level and if so, the details thereof;
- (d) whether cluster-based food processing models are being encouraged and if so, the details thereof;
- (e) whether packaging and quality certification support exists and if so, the details thereof; and
- (f) whether Bhiwani-Mahendergarh Lok Sabha Constituency would be selected for a farmer- led food-processing entrepreneurship cluster and if so, the details thereof?

## ANSWER

THE MINISTER OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES (SHRI CHIRAG PASWAN)

(a) to (f): A statement is laid on the Table of the House.

*****

## STATEMENT REFERRED TO IN REPLY TO PART (a) TO (f) OF LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. *185 ANSWERED ON 12.02.2026 REGARDING 'FARMER-OWNED FOOD PROCESSING STARTUPS'

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 185 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Shri Dharambir Singh
Subject: Farmer-owned Food Processing Startups
---

(a) Ministry  of  Food  Processing  Industries  (MoFPI)  is  implementing  various  schemes  aimed  at enhancing  food  processing  capacities,  promoting  value-added  products,  fostering  innovation,  and improving returns to farmers and entrepreneurs. These include Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro food processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme, Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY) and the Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing  Industry (PLISFPI). Under these schemes, subsidies, incentives and credit support are available to eligible entities including startups, micro enterprises, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Self Help Groups (SHGs) and individual entrepreneurs  in  food  processing.  Under  the  PMFME  scheme,  support  for  setting  up  of  Incubation Centre  is  provided  to  Government  Institutes/Organizations  and  Private Agencies.  These  Incubation Centres  provide  support  for  product  development,  Lab  testing,  and  training  support  to  micro  food processing entrepreneurs, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and Startups.

NIFTEM Technology Innovation and Business Incubation Foundation (NTIBIF) of National Institute of Food  Technology  Entrepreneurship  &  Management,  Kundli,  Haryana  (NIFTEM-K)  and  NIFTEM, Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu (NIFTEM-T) offers incubation facilities and supports startups in the food and agri sectors, as well as Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, student entrepreneurs, research-based ventures, and FPOs.

Till date, NTIBIF has incubated 55 startups across the country, disbursing seed grants worth nearly ₹1.19 crore. These  ventures  span  areas  such  as  Ready  to  Eat/  Ready  to  Cook  products,  functional  foods, alternative proteins, and supply chain innovation. Besides, NIFTEM-T has also supported / handholded 40 MSMEs startups.

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 185 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Shri Dharambir Singh
Subject: Farmer-owned Food Processing Startups
---

MoFPI has also organised a Startup Conclave titled SUFALAM (Startup Forum for Aspiring Leaders and Mentors) through its autonomous institution, National Institute of Food Technology Entrepreneurship  &  Management,  Kundli,  Haryana  (NIFTEM-K)  to  promote  startups  in  the  food processing sector.  Further, MoFPI during its flagship event World Food India has organised Startup Grand Challenge during 2023, 2024 and 2025 editions in association with NIFTEMs.  The winners of Start-up Grand Challenge are provided with cash awards and incubation support through NIFTEM.

(b) To support rural entrepreneurs in branding, packaging, and market access, the PMFME Scheme provides support for branding and marketing to the groups of FPOs/ SHGs/ Cooperatives or Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) of micro food processing enterprises with a grant of upto 50% of the eligible project cost following the cluster approach for developing a common brand with provision for quality control, standardisation, packaging and adhering to food safety parameters for consumer retail sale.

In addition to this, Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD) has registered three trademarks as "Saras", 'Saras Aajeevika" and "Aajeevika" for branding and marketing of products of SHGs. Further, marketing platforms such as periodic SARAS Fairs at the National and State levels, the SARAS Gallery and the ecommerce portal (www.esaras.in) have been established to enhance market access and visibility of SHG products.

(c) The PMFME Scheme supports establishment and strengthening of Common Incubation Centre, which function as local hubs for processing, training, testing, and entrepreneurship development. Each incubation centre has atleast one processing line in One District One Product (ODOP) and 3-5 processing lines for allied products and operates on a custom-hiring basis. As on date, 80 Incubation centres have been approved across 27 States/ UTs.

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 185 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Shri Dharambir Singh
Subject: Farmer-owned Food Processing Startups
---

Also, the Ministry through NIFTEMs provide incubation support to food processing start-ups, FPOs, MSMEs, and rural entrepreneurs, with a strong focus on value addition, entrepreneurship development, and commercialization.

The Ministry of Rural Development through Small Village Entrepreneurship Programme (SVEP) which is  an  umbrella  scheme  of  Deendayal Antyodaya  Yojana  -  National  Rural  Livelihoods  Mission  is providing demand-based incubator program for supporting SHG enterprises.

(d) The  Ministry  of  Food  Processing  Industries  (MoFPI)  has  been  implementing  a  Scheme  for Creation of Infrastructure for Agro Processing Clusters (APC), a component scheme of the Pradhan Mantri  Kisan  Sampada  Yojana  (PMKSY)  for  development  of  modern  infrastructure  to  encourage entrepreneurs to set up food processing units based on cluster approach in the country. The Scheme envisages grants-in-aid @35% of eligible project cost for projects in general areas and @50% of eligible project cost of projects in Difficult Areas (North East States including Sikkim, State of Uttarakhand, State of Himachal Pradesh, Union Territories of J&K and Ladakh, State notified ITDP (Integrated Tribal Development Projects) areas and Islands (Union Territories of Andaman & Nicobar and Lakshadweep) as well as for projects of SC/ST, FPOs and SHGs entrepreneurs subject to maximum of Rs. 10.00 crore.

In addition to this, Horticulture Cluster Development Programme (CDP) is implemented by the National Horticulture Board (NHB), Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare (MoA&FW). The programme has been structured to promote integrated, end-to-end value-chain development with a strong focus on post-harvest management, food processing, and market integration.

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 185 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Shri Dharambir Singh
Subject: Farmer-owned Food Processing Startups
---

(e) Under the branding and marketing component of the PMFME Scheme, support is offered for development of common brand. The on-boarded micro-enterprises receive comprehensive training in product development, quality assurance, ingredient standards, packaging, and labelling in compliance with Food Safety & Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) regulations. In addition to this, formalization of micro food processing enterprises is facilitated by District Resource Persons (DRPs) through Udyam Aadhaar registration, FSSAI registration, GST registration as per the applicability.

(f) The PMFME Scheme is demand driven and is implemented through State/ UT Governments. Any interested individual/entity may avail the benefit for upgradation of existing or setting up of new micro-food processing facility, in accordance with the scheme guidelines. Till 31 st December 2025, loans for 97 micro food processing enterprises have been sanctioned in Bhiwani and Mahendergarh districts of Haryana.

The component schemes under PMKSY including APC are demand driven and proposals are invited by floating Expression of Interest (EoI) from time to time.

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 186 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: TRIBAL AFFAIRS
Asked by: Smt. Priyanka Gandhi Vadra
Subject: Status of PM-JANMAN in Kerala
---

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS

## LOK SABHA

## STARRED QUESTION NO-*186

TO BE ANSWERED ON-12/02/2026

## STATUS OF PM-JANMAN IN KERALA

- *186. SMT. PRIYANKA GANDHI VADRA:

Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:

- (a) whether the Government has evaluated the progress of Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN) in Kerala and if so, the details thereof including  district-wise details of projects sanctioned and completed;
- (b) whether the Government proposes to take action to address gaps in the implementation of PMJANMAN, particularly delays in completion of houses under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana in PMJANMAN villages in Kerala and if so, the details thereof;
- (c) the details of the funds sanctioned and released under PM-JANMAN in Kerala;
- (d)  the  details  of  PM-JANMAN  projects  sanctioned  and  completed  in  Wayanad  Lok  Sabha Constituency; and
- (e) whether the Government has received complaints from Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) beneficiaries regarding delays in completion of projects due to non-release of sanctioned funds in Wayanad Lok Sabha Constituency and if so, the details thereof and the action taken thereon?

## ANSWER

MINISTER OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI JUAL ORAM)

- (a) to (e): A statement is laid on the table of the House.

Statement referred to in reply to parts (a) to (e) of Lok Sabha Starred Question No. *186 for answer on  12.02.2026 raised  by Smt.  Priyanka  Gandhi  Vadra  regarding  'Status  of  PMJANMAN in Kerala'

(a), (b) & (d):  The details of progress of interventions under PM JANMAN in Kerala, districtwise  including  Wayanad,  Kozhikode  and  Malappuram  districts  of  Wayanad  Lok  Sabha Constituency, are given in Annexure-I .

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 186 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: TRIBAL AFFAIRS
Asked by: Smt. Priyanka Gandhi Vadra
Subject: Status of PM-JANMAN in Kerala
---

To ensure the effective implementation and monitoring of the PM JANMAN, Ministry has been regularly  reviewing  the  implementation  with  State  Governments  and  District  administrations. Besides, for monitoring the progress of the works under PM JANMAN, digital dashboard on PM GatiShakti portal has been developed. A Project Monitoring Unit in the Ministry has also been established for monitoring and coordination with Ministries and States / UT. Further, as informed by State Government of Kerala, it is being encouraged to support the construction of PMAY-G houses through convergence of state-level funding (LIFE Mission) to meet the high construction costs in hilly terrains.

(c)  The  details  of  financial  progress  for  interventions  of  Ministry  of  Tribal  Affairs  under  the abhiyan are given in Annexure-II .

(e) No, Sir.

Annex referred to in reply to part (a), (b) & (d) of Lok Sabha Starred Question No.*186 for answer on  12.02.2026 raised  by Smt.  Priyanka  Gandhi  Vadra  regarding  'Status  of  PMJANMAN in Kerala'

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 187 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: CIVIL AVIATION
Asked by: Shri Dushyant Singh
Subject: Emergence of India as Global Aviation Hub
---

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION

## LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. : 187

( TO BE ANSWERED ON THE  12th February 2026 )

## EMERGENCE OF INDIA AS GLOBAL AVIATION HUB

187.    SHRI DUSHYANT SINGH Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION

be pleased to state:-

- (a)  whether the  Government has assessed current capacity and preparedness of the country to emerge as a global aviation hub in view of the significant fleet expansion undertaken  by  Indian  carriers  and  the  growing  volume  of  international  air  traffic transiting over Indian airspace and if so, the details thereof;
- (b)  the  details  of  the  steps  taken  to  strengthen  airport  infrastructure,  including development  of  hub  airports,  regional  connectivity  under  the  UDAN  scheme  and enhancement of multimodal connectivity at major airports during the last three years;
- (c) whether the Government has taken any measures to promote domestic Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul (MRO) capabilities and reduce dependence on overseas facilities and if so, the details thereof; and
- (d)  the  initiatives  undertaken  by  the  Government  to  address  skill  development, workforce training and capacity building in the civil aviation sector to support longterm growth?

## ANSWER

Minister of CIVIL AVIATION                             (Shri Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu) (a) to (d): A Statement is laid on the Table of the House.

******

STATEMENT REFERRED TO IN REPLY TO PARTS (A) TO (D) IN RESPECT OF LOK  SABHA  STARRED  QUESTION  NO.  187  FOR  ANSWER  ON  12.02.2026 REGARDING  "EMERGENCE  OF  INDIA  AS  GLOBAL  AVIATION  HUB"  BY SHRI DUSHYANT SINGH

(a): As per the National Civil Aviation Policy, 2016, India has the potential to be among the global top three nations in terms of domestic and international passenger traffic. For the development of aviation hubs, this Ministry has formulated an International Aviation Hub Strategy in consultation with stakeholders.

meta.subject: Emergence of India as Global Aviation Hub

text:
Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 187 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: CIVIL AVIATION
Asked by: Shri Dushyant Singh
Subject: Emergence of India as Global Aviation Hub
---

(b):  The  modernisation  and  expansion  of  airport  infrastructure  is  being  carried  out through upgradation of existing airports and development of greenfield airports. To improve the airport infrastructure in the country, Airports Authority of India and PPP Partners have incurred CAPEX of more than Rs. 96000 crore during FY 2019-20 to FY 2024-25. Further, RCS-UDAN is a market driven scheme in which the unserved and  underserved  airstrips/airports  are  listed  for  bidding  by  airlines  to  provide connectivity.  As  on  20.01.2026,  a  total  of  657  routes  have  been  operationalised, connecting 93 unserved and underserved airports, including 15 heliports and 2 water aerodromes.  The  Government  provides  for  integrated  planning  for  multimodal connectivity at major airports as a transformative approach for last mile connectivity under the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan (PMGS NMP).

(c): The Government has introduced measures to boost India's aircraft MRO sector, including  new  guidelines  abolishing  royalties,  transparent  land  allotments,  reduced IGST and GST rates with full input tax credits, extended timelines for export and reimport of goods for repairs, etc. The Visa Manual has also been amended to incorporate suitable provisions for granting Business Visa and Temporary Landing Permit (TLP) to foreign pilots and crew of scheduled or non-scheduled flights coming for the purpose of MRO services in India.

(d):  The Government of India has taken various initiatives towards building aviation skill  development  ecosystem  in  the  country  such  as  issuance  of  liberalised  Flying Training Organisation (FTO) Policy, revision of Civil Aviation Requirements (CAR) to expedite FTO approvals, enhancing training quality, standardising benchmarks and optimising capacity. DGCA has issued CAR-147(Basic) to streamline the syllabus and skilled training requirements for the development of competent/ skilled manpower for the maintenance of aircraft.

******

meta.subject: Emergence of India as Global Aviation Hub

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS

## LOK SABHA

## STARRED QUESTION NO. 189 TO BE ANSWERED ON FEBRUARY 12, 2026

## UTILIZATION OF FUNDS UNDER THE SMART CITIES MISSION

NO. 189.    SHRI E T MOHAMMED BASHEER:

Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:

- (a) whether  the  Government  has  assessed  the  total  quantum  of  unutilised Central  and  State  funds  under  the  Smart  Cities  Mission  and  if  so,  the
- details thereof; and
- (b) the steps taken or proposed to be taken by the Government to ensure longterm  maintenance,  sustainable  operational  funding  and  integration  of Integrated  Command  and  Control  Centres  into  the  municipal  governance
- framework of all the hundred Smart Cities?

## ANSWER

## THE MINISTER OF HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS (SHRI MANOHAR LAL)

- (a) & (b):  A statement is laid on the Table of the House.

STATEMENT  REFERRED  TO  IN  REPLY  OF  PARTS  (a)  &  (b)  OF  THE  LOK SABHA  STARRED  QUESTION  NO.  189  FOR  ANSWER  ON  12.02.2026 REGARDING UTILIZATION OF FUNDS UNDER THE SMART CITIES MISSION

- (a) Under Smart Cities Mission (SCM), out of Central Government's allocation of  ₹48,000  Cr,  the  cities  have  been  able  to  claim  central  financial assistance of ₹47,458 Cr (i.e. 99% of the total Central share allocation for cities) of which of ₹46,231 Cr has been utilized (i.e. 97% of the total Central share allocation for cities).

Post closure of SCM on 31.03.2025, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)  has  issued  Advisory  No.  27  dated  10 th   June  2025.  As  per  the advisory, the SPV are to ensure submission of their Utilization Certificates (UCs) for the GOI funds released to them not later than March 2026.

City wise details of the Central Financial Assistance claimed and utilized, State fund claimed and utilized are at Annexure.

meta.subject: Utilization of Funds under the Smart Cities Mission

text:
Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 189 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS
Asked by: Shri E T Mohammed Basheer
Subject: Utilization of Funds under the Smart Cities Mission
---

- (b)  The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) has issued advisories and conducted periodic review meetings to ensure the long-term maintenance and sustainable operational funding of Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs). As per Advisory No. 27 dated 10 th  June 2025, all  SPVs are to ensure the timely completion of all ongoing SCM projects and  prepare  O&M  plans  for  the  assets  created.  The  Advisory  has  further outlined the envisioned future role of SPVs of smart cities and a roadmap for the continued functioning of ICCCs. The advisory underscores repurposing  of  SPVs  to  meet  emerging  urban  challenges.  Further,  as  per the  advisory,  ICCCs  are  to  be  leveraged  as  city  operating  systems  and state  analytical  hubs,  with  operational  control  preferably  retained  by  the urban development departments. SPVs may receive service-linked revenues  for  managing  ICCC  functions.  States  and  Union  Territories, through their respective High-Powered Steering Committees (HPSCs), have been encouraged to chart out urban sector priorities and map areas where SPVs can continue to play a strategic role.

Additionally, Advisory No. 29 on Outcomes of the National Conference on 'Making  Cities  Cybersecure'  encourages  ICCCs  towards  cyber  resilience and financial sustainability. This includes developing models to ensure the financial sustainability of ICCCs, monetizing existing assets, exploring the possibility of sharing revenue and innovative financing models.

MoHUA  has  encouraged  ICCCs  towards  integration  of  line  departments within  the  municipal  governance  and  has  also  recommended  ICCCs  to render services to the line departments of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). The ICCCs support municipal governance through integration with ULB systems for solid waste management,  water  supply and sanitation, disaster management,  health,  energy,  environmental,  e-governance  and  grievance redressal to ensure efficient monitoring and delivery of municipal services to citizens.

Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Dr. Amol Ramsing Kolhe
Subject: Financial Assistance to Food Processing Units in Shirur
---

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES

## LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. *18

ANSWERED ON 29 TH  JANUARY, 2026

## FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO FOOD PROCESSING UNITS IN SHIRUR

## *18. DR. AMOL RAMSING KOLHE:

Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:

- (a) the total financial assistance and technical support provided to Food Processing Units and agri-businesses in the Shirur Lok Sabha Constituency of Maharashtra during the last five years;
- (b) the number of jobs created by the food processing sector in the said region and the measures being taken to encourage further investment therein;
- (c) the details of any new schemes or policies being framed by the Government to promote processing of agricultural produce and to reduce post-harvest losses; and
- (d) whether the Government proposes to establish food parks and special economic zones for the food processing sector in Maharashtra and if so, the details thereof?

## ANSWER

## THE MINISTER OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES (SHRI CHIRAG PASWAN)

- (a) to (d): A statement is laid on the Table of the House.

*****

## STATEMENT  REFERRED  TO  IN  REPLY  TO  PARTS  (a)  TO  (d)  OF  STARRED QUESTION  NO.  *18  FOR  ANSWER  ON  29 TH   JANUARY,  2026  REGARDING 'FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO FOOD PROCESSING UNITS IN SHIRUR'

(a) : Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has approved 4 food processing units with approved grants in aid of Rs 30.46 crores under Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY), 8 projects with committed investment of Rs 165.88 crores under Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) and 1386 micro food processing units  with  subsidy  of  Rs  76.69  crores  under  PM  Formalization  of  Micro  Food  Processing Enterprises (PMFME) scheme during the last 5 years in the Pune district of Maharashtra.

Further,  PMFME  scheme  also  provides  handhold  support  for  formalization  and operationalization of the micro food processing enterprises.

meta.subject: Financial Assistance to Food Processing Units in Shirur

text:
Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 18 | STARRED | Date: 29.01.2026
Ministry: FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
Asked by: Dr. Amol Ramsing Kolhe
Subject: Financial Assistance to Food Processing Units in Shirur
---

(b) & (c): MoFPI provides subsidy/incentives to the eligible applicants through the schemes implemented  and  thereby  creating  off-farm  employment  opportunities  in  Food  Processing Sector.  As on 31 st December, 2025, 10397 direct/ indirect employment opportunities have been  generated  from  the  operational  projects  under  component  schemes  of  PMKSY,  4158 direct and indirect employment opportunities has been generated under PMFME scheme and 7355 direct/ indirect employment opportunities has been generated under PLISFPI in the Pune district of Maharashtra.

In order to boost and ensure overall development of Food Processing Industries, MoFPI has been incentivizing setting up/expansion of related infrastructure through its two Central Sector  Schemes  namely  PMKSY  and  PLISFPI.  Further,  a  Centrally  sponsored  PMFME scheme is also being implemented by MoFPI. All the three schemes are implemented across the country including Shirur Lok Sabha Constituency of Maharashtra.

These schemes aim towards creation of modern infrastructure with efficient supply chain management from farm gate to retail outlet which includes storage, transportation, value addition  etc.,  thereby  help  in  providing  better  returns  to  farmers,  reducing  wastage  of agricultural produce, reduction in post harvest losses, enhancing productivity and increasing the processing level.

(d): Schemes implemented by MoFPI are not region specific but demand driven and MoFPI does not establish food processing units on its own. However, it provides financial assistance in  the  form  of  grants-in-aid  to  eligible  entrepreneurs for  establishing  related  infrastructure. Mega  Food  Park  and  Creation  of  Infrastructure  for  Agro-processing  Clusters  (APC) components of PMKSY are aimed at development of such Industries in clusters. The Mega Food Park Scheme has been discontinued by the Ministry since 01.04.2021. So far, MoFPI has approved 3 Mega Food Parks and 13 APC in Maharashtra.

*****

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

## LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. 190

## ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026

SAFETY LAPSES DURING CONSTRUCTION OF NH-66 IN KERALA *190.    SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:

Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

सड़क परिवहन औि िाजमार्ग मंत्री

be pleased to state:

- (a) the details of all accidents, mishaps and fatal incidents reported during the ongoing  construction  and  widening  works  of  National  Highway-66  up  to 15.01.2026 in Kerala, district-wise;
- (b) the number of deaths and serious injuries reported in the said accidents, separately indicating passengers/road users and construction workers;
- (c) the names of construction companies, concessionaires and subcontractors involved in the respective stretches where such accidents occurred;
- (d)  the  findings  of  safety  audits  or  inquiries  conducted  into  these  incidents and the lapses identified therein;
- (e)  the  action  taken  against  the  agencies  concerned  including  penalties imposed, termination of contracts or blacklisting; and
- (f)  the  steps  taken  to  strengthen  safety  measures,  traffic  management  and worker protection during construction of NH-66 to prevent recurrence of such incidents?

## ANSWER

THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

(SHRI NITIN JAIRAM GADKARI)

- (a) to (f)        A statement is laid on the Table of the House.

STATEMENT REFERRED TO IN REPLY TO PARTS (a)  TO  (f)  OF  LOK  SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. 190 ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026 ASKED BY SHRI  KODIKUNNIL  SURESH REGARDING  SAFETY  LAPSES  DURING CONSTRUCTION OF NH-66 IN KERALA.

Asked by: Shri Kodikunnil Suresh
Subject: Safety Lapses during Construction of NH-66 in Kerala
---

(a) to (f) The summary of accidents, mishaps and fatal incidents reported at construction sites of ongoing projects on NH-66 is annexed.   Lapses mainly related  to  non-adherence  to  overall  Traffic  Management,  Work  Zone  Safety, and  Construction  Safety  Measures,  non-compliances  in  the  areas  of  traffic management,  barricading,  signage,  diversion  implementation,  road  surface condition, protection for vulnerable road users, worker safety practices, and night-time visibility, has been found in Road Safety Audit.

ANNEXURE REFERRED TO IN REPLY TO PART (a) to (f) OF LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. 190 ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026 ASKED BY SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH REGARDING 'SAFETY LAPSES DURING CONSTRUCTION OF NH-66 IN KERALA '.

text:
Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 191 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS
Asked by: Dr. Anand Kumar
Subject: Widening of Barabanki-Bahraich-Rupaidiha NH-927
---

## GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

## LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. 191 ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026

## WIDENING OF BARABANKI-BAHRAICH-RUPAIDIHA NH-927

†*191.  DR. ANAND KUMAR:

Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

सड़क परिवहन औि िाजमार्ग मंत्री

be pleased to state:

- (a)  whether a Detailed Project Report (DPR) has been prepared for widening the two-lane Barabanki-Bahraich-Rupaidiha section of National Highway No. 927 into a four-lane highway;
- (b) if so, the details thereof including the total length of the proposed four-lane National Highway;
- (c) whether the said National Highway is proposed to be developed as the first Digital Highway of Uttar Pradesh;
- (d)  if  so,  the  details  thereof  and  the  salient  features  of  the  proposed  Digital Highway; and
- (e)  the  time  by  which  the  widening  of  the  said  National  Highway  and construction  of  a  bridge  over  the  Ghagra  river  is  likely  to  commence  along with timeline set for completion of the said works?

## ANSWER

THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS

(SHRI NITIN JAIRAM GADKARI)

- (a) to (e)        A statement is laid on the Table of the House.

STATEMENT REFERRED TO IN REPLY TO PARTS (a) TO (e) OF LOK SABHA STARRED QUESTION NO. 191 ANSWERED ON 12 TH  FEBRUARY, 2026 ASKED BY  DR.  ANAND  KUMAR  REGARDING  WIDENING  OF  BARABANKI-BAHRAICHRUPAIDIHA NH-927.

- (a)  &  (b)  Yes  Sir.  The  Detailed  Project  Report  (DPR)  for  four-laning  of  the Barabanki-Bahraich section has been finalized in two packages. The details of the two packages are as follows:

Lok Sabha: 18 | Session: 7 | Q: 191 | STARRED | Date: 12.02.2026
Ministry: ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS
Asked by: Dr. Anand Kumar
Subject: Widening of Barabanki-Bahraich-Rupaidiha NH-927
---

|   S.No | Project name                                        | Length (Km.)   |
|--------|-----------------------------------------------------|----------------|
|      1 | Barabanki to Mushfabad from Km.0.000 to Km.43.030   | 43.030 Km.     |
|      2 | Mustfabad to Biswariya from Km.43.030 to Km.101.515 | 58.485 Km.     |

Further, the feasibility study for 4-laning from Bahraich to Rupaidiha has been awarded.

- (c) & (d) Advanced Traffic Management System (ATMS) shall be installed. The ATMS is having features of Variable Messaging Sign Board (VMSB), Accident detection  system  with  cameras,  weather  monitoring  system,  optical  fibre network etc., which shall be installed on the Project Highway as part of the project.
- (e) Scheduled completion of project will be 24 months after the award of work and declaration of Appointed Date, post sanction of project

*****
